description:
Goal
Make `compiler2::port_codegen_test::enum_tier0_fixture_exercises_basic_operations` bounded. The test is currently a TODO that ignores `drive()` result, but it still hangs for more than 90s and can stall the wider `cargo test --lib compiler2::` run.

Signal
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 90s cargo test --lib compiler2::port_codegen_test::enum_tier0_fixture_exercises_basic_operations -- --exact --nocapture` exits before timeout.
- The test either asserts the intended resolved behavior or explicitly records a bounded unresolved outcome with a narrower follow-up; it must not silently ignore an unbounded drive.
- Telemetry gives a compact event budget and identifies the remaining Enum operation if resolution is still not complete.

Strategy
Run the fixture under telemetry, identify the repeated activation/callsite frontier, and either finish the missing Enum semantic path or split a smaller blocker. Do not solve by increasing timeouts or leaving the TODO as an unbounded smoke test.

Outro
No unbounded TODO test, no swallowed drive hang, no fixture-wide special case, and no broad top/bottom readiness sentinel.
title: Compiler2 Enum tier0: replace hanging TODO drive with bounded convergence evidence
